vllm.title: [Perf]:Optimize qwen2-vl to reduce cudaMemcpyAsync
vllm.author: cynthieye
vllm.bodyExcerpt: qwen2-vl logic optimization: During each forward propagation, the xformer branch of Qwen2VisionTransformer will execute multiple tensor tolist methods (flash attn branch will execute multiple tensor items) to force the GPU tensor to be copied to the CPU, triggering CUDAMemcpyAsync to increase time c

vllm.title: benchmarks: simplify test jsonschema
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
benchmarks: Simplify test jsonschema
PR #13288 changed this schema from an object to an array. The real issue
was that the original schema used features unsupported by xgrammar.
However, by switching to an array, I saw the failure rate go up a lot in
my benchmark runs. This is because as an unbounde

vllm.title: dynamic distpatch of fp8 kernels
vllm.author: jeffdaily
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This mostly affects ROCm with hardware that can support one or the other FP8 type. All fp8 kernels are now templated on fp8_type instead of assuming a single fp8_type via using FP8_TYPE = . CUDA is largely unaffected.  For CUDA, fp8 kernels only instantiate the OCP type; no binary bloat. For ROCm, t

vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt: The is_causal config has been flipped to False on the HF repo, so we need to update our docs and tests accordingly.

vllm.title: Uninstall dependencies before installing requirements/tpu.txt
vllm.author: richardsliu
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Uninstall torch, torch_xla and torch_vision before installing from requirements/tpu.txt to prevent version conflicts.

vllm.title: [V1] Add regex structured output support with xgrammar
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
[V1] Add regex structured output support with xgrammar
This is built on top of #14563, as the upgraded xgrammar is required for regex
support to work.
Signed-off-by: Russell Bryant rbryant@redhat.com

vllm.title: docs: Add documentation for s390x cpu implementation

vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR provides documentation for building  vLLM cpu implementation for s390x architecture. PR for s390x cpu implementation is approved and awaiting merge. #12613

vllm.title: [BugFix/Build] Fix sparse kernels not getting built on hopper
vllm.author: LucasWilkinson
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
#14354 caused the sparse kernels to not get built on hopper resulting in:
NotImplementedError: No compiled cutlass_sparse_compress for a compute capability less than CUDA device capability: 90

Tested using:
vllm serve nm-testing/TinyLlama-1.1B-Chat-v1.0-sparse2of4_fp8_dynamic-e2e

vllm.title: [Hardware][Intel GPU] upgrade IPEX dependency to 2.6.10.
vllm.author: jikunshang
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
IPEX-xpu released 2.6.10 version recently, this PR upgrade ipex dependency to latest version.
There are some new features in ipex-xpu 2.6.10, like chunked_prefill kernel, lora, etc. will integrate these new features into vllm in further PRs.

vllm.title: [V1] Remove cache from StructuredOutputManager
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This change simplifies the code in StrucutredOutputManager by removing
the cache. It turns out this is not necessary since xgrammar does its
own caching. This simplification is a nice prerequisite to simplify
things a bit before refactoring this further to support multiple
backends.
Signed-off-by: R

vllm.title: [V1] Delay all xgrammar usage until needed
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
PR #14575 delayed initialization of the grammar bitmask until it was
needed to try to fix a problem encountered on TPU systems.
Unfortunately, that change was not sufficient.
We need to delay usage of ALL xgrammar APIs, not just the grammar
initialization. This change implements that. More initializ

vllm.title: Fix run_tpu_test
vllm.author: richardsliu
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Remove a test that's no longer there.

vllm.title: [V1][TPU] Pad the block_table.shape[1] so the ragged paged attention can handle correctly
vllm.author: vanbasten23
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
We uncovered an ragged paged attention constraint that will cause trouble if it is not handle properly. The kernel assumes that block_table.shape[1]%NUM_KV_PAGES_PER_BLOCK==0. If this constraint is violated, there may be out-of-bound issue and the kernel will crash.
While we are fixing the kernel in

vllm.bodyExcerpt: These 2 log files kept growing and would block TPU release job at some point.

vllm.title: [Feature] Add `vllm bench` CLI
vllm.author: randyjhc
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Part of #13840
This PR adds CLI commands of vllm bench. We only support vllm bench serve in this PR to align the interface, the following benchmark modes will be added in follow-up PRs (contributions are welcome).
vllm bench latency
vllm bench throughput

What has been covered in this PR:

All metri

vllm.title: [core][V1] pluggable scheduler
vllm.author: joerunde
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
While we wait for the scheduler interface overhaul on #12544, this PR aims to only make the scheduler for the V1 engine pluggable. This allows the --scheduler-cls engine arg to continue to function the same way it does in V0.
We require this for the vllm-spyre plugin, which overrides the scheduler t

vllm.title: [Doc] Update benchmarks README
vllm.author: JenZhao
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Update the benchmarks README to better reflect the latest dataset changes from #14036

vllm.title: [Model] Extend Ultravox to accept audio longer than 30s
vllm.author: farzadab
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Currently the Ultravox model input is capped to 30 seconds and extra audio is truncated (AFAIK). Also each sample is fed to Whisper individually (without being batched).
This PR allows using longer audio by chunking them first, using Whisper encoder in batch mode, and then concatenates them.

vllm.title: [V1][Bugfix][Spec Decode] Fix incorrect outputs in V1 speculative decoding due to batch indexing
vllm.author: benchislett
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
In gpu_model_runner.py, the requests in the batch are ordered according to self.input_batch.req_ids. However, the speculative decoding code is inferring the batch order from the keys of the dictionary scheduler_output.num_scheduled_tokens which is not necessarily ordered in the same way.
When multip
